content.problemStatement:
###Read problems statements Bengali and Vietnamese as well.

Ghayeeth is a math teacher. He likes playing games, so he invented a new game which he wants to play against his student Siroj. The rules of the game are as follows:


Initially, Ghayeeth writes the numbers  A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ on a whiteboard and a number Z=0Z=0Z=0 on a piece of paper.
Ghayeeth and Siroj alternate turns; Ghayeeth goes first.
In each turn, the current player must pick a number that is currently written on the whiteboard, add it to ZZZ and erase it from the whiteboard.
After each turn, if there is no pair of positive integers (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) such that Z=a2−b2Z = a^2 - b^2Z=a2−b2, then the last player who made a move loses the game.
Otherwise, if there are no more numbers on the whiteboard, the last player who made a move wins the game.
Help Ghayeeth determine the winner of this game.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string 'Ghayeeth' or 'Siroj' — the name of the winner.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, Ghayeeth can win by choosing 7, then Siroj chooses 9, and Ghayeeth chooses 11. For the second test case, Siroj can win by choosing 13, then Ghayeeth chooses 15.
Ghayeeth can win by choosing 10, then Siroj chooses 20.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to determine the winner of the game based on the given rules. We can use a recursive approach to simulate the game and determine the winner. The key idea is to check if there is a pair of positive integers (a, b) such that Z = a^2 - b^2 after each turn. If there is no such pair, the last player who made a move loses the game.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * sqrt(max(Ai))) — explain: per query is O(N * sqrt(max(Ai))), total is O(T * N * sqrt(max(Ai)))
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — explain what dominates: the space complexity is dominated by the recursive call stack and the space needed to store the input array
